title: TikTok-style full-screen video feed (one card per screen)

requirements.summary: Redesign the main feed (HomePage) so each video post card occupies exactly one full screen height with scroll-snap enabled
acceptanceCriteria: Each video card in the main feed takes up the full visible screen height below the header.
Scrolling snaps so exactly one card is visible at a time — no partial card is visible.
The video player and card content (avatar, title, actions) all fit within the single card without overflowing or being cut off.
The header remains accessible and does not push card content out of view.
Works correctly on mobile viewport sizes (as shown in the uploaded screenshot).

file_operations.description: Wrap the video post cards in a scroll-snap container with snap-type: y mandatory. Set the container height to calc(100dvh - header height) or 100dvh if the header overlays, and set each card to min-height: 100% with scroll-snap-align: start. Remove any existing grid or flex layouts that show multiple cards side-by-side. Ensure the video post loading skeleton respects the same full-height layout.

Refactor VideoPostCard to occupy the full available height (min-height: 100%) within its parent scroll-snap container. Position the video element to fill the card background, and overlay the creator avatar, title, like/reply/share actions, and delete button using absolute positioning or a flexbox stack. Ensure all interactive elements are visible and accessible without overflow. Apply scroll-snap-align: start to the card root. Adjust spacing, padding, and z-index so text and actions remain readable over the video content.

Add global CSS utility classes or custom properties to support full-screen card layouts, such as .snap-container { scroll-snap-type: y mandatory; overflow-y: scroll; } and .snap-item { scroll-snap-align: start; scroll-snap-stop: always; }. Ensure dvh units are supported for mobile browsers and provide a fallback to vh where necessary. Optionally add smooth scrolling if desired.
